entities.name: Mini-model orchestration
entities.context: Architectural approach favoring multiple specialized smaller models over single frontier models for resilience and cost-effectiveness

entities.name: Swiss Army Knife fallacy
entities.context: Named principle that one tool for everything is often less effective than specialized tools

entities.name: Modularity
entities.context: Architectural principle reducing single points of failure, enabling easier debugging and component upgrades

entities.name: Agentic orchestration
entities.context: Implementation approach for coordinating AI systems as discrete logical steps

entities.name: Prompt-chaining
entities.context: Technique for connecting multiple smaller, faster model calls in sequence

entities.name: Frontier model
entities.context: Single cutting-edge LLM treated as black-box oracle; contrasted with modular approach

entities.name: Unit economics
entities.context: Business consideration favoring orchestrated mini-models for better cost structure

relationships.context: Provides theoretical reasoning for preferring specialized models over monolithic ones
Both favor logical structure and component isolation over brute-force intelligence
Complementary implementation techniques for the mini-model architecture
Architectural alternative that reduces dependency on any single provider
Modular architecture protects against commoditization and ensures better cost structure
